subject: Cloud Computing
title: Right-Sizing Resources
description: A short lesson to help you apply Right-Sizing Resources.

hook.text: A team runs 50 servers with 16 CPUs each. Average utilization: 5%. They're paying for 95% empty space.

buildup.text: Right-sizing means matching resource allocation to actual usage. Use smaller instances when demand is low.

discovery.text: Cloud providers offer tools to analyze usage: AWS Compute Optimizer, Azure Advisor, GCP Recommender.

twist.text: Teams resist right-sizing because smaller instances feel risky. 'What if we need the capacity?' they say.

climax.text: Combining right-sizing with auto-scaling gives you both: low costs normally and capacity when you need it.

punchline.text: Don't pay for resources you're not using. Right-size first.

quiz.question: What is right-sizing in cloud cost management?
options: Matching resource allocation to actual usage patterns to eliminate waste
Always choosing the largest available instance for maximum performance
Shutting down all cloud resources during off-peak hours
